hypothesis: Branched and cross-linked polyamine sorbents will exhibit higher CO2 uptake than equivalently amine-dense linear polymers under ambient humidity at 0.4 mbar CO2 due to enhanced micro-porosity and cooperative water–amine–CO2 interactions, and a two-fidelity Bayesian optimization framework that jointly leverages fast coarse-grained MD and selective all-atom MD/GCMC will identify these branched candidates more rapidly than single-fidelity searches on linear polymers.
